name: 1A
easement: The following easements apply on Greater Anglia services from Monday to Friday inclusive: 08:20 Clacton-on Sea to London Liverpool Street service - from Clacton, Thorpe-le-Soken, Weeley, Great Bentley, Alresford, Wivenhoe and Hythe, to all destinations. 08:01 Walton-on-the-Naze to London Liverpool Street service, from Walton, Frinton-on-Sea and Kirby Cross to all destinations. 08:08 Harwich Town to Colchester service - from all stations to all destinations. 09:02 London Liverpool Street to Ipswich service - from Liverpool Street and Stratford (London) to Chappel & Wakes Colne, Bures and Sudbury (Suffolk) only, changing at Marks Tey. 09:18 London Liverpool Street to Clacton-on-Sea service - from Liverpool Street and Stratford (London) to Wivenhoe, Thorpe-le-Soken, Clacton-on-Sea, Kirby Cross, Frinton-on-Sea and Walton-on-the-Naze. 09:28 London Liverpool Street to Cambridge North service, to all destinations. 18:30 London Liverpool Street to Norwich service - from Liverpool Street to Derby Road (Ipswich), Trimley and Felixstowe only, changing at Ipswich.
seasonalVariations: School Holidays - Evening peak restrictions on return journeys from London Liverpool Street and Stratford (London) (which only apply to Ipswich and beyond) and from Liverpool Street and Tottenham Hale (which only apply to Brandon and beyond via Cambridge) are lifted during school holidays and half-term weeks. Note that restrictions from London Kings Cross are not affected and remain in place. 2024 Summer: Monday 22 July to Tuesday 3 September Autumn Half-Term: Friday 25 October to Friday 1 November Christmas and New Year: Monday 23 December to Friday 3 January 2025. 2025 Half-Term: Monday 17 to Friday 21 February Easter: Monday 7 to Tuesday 22 April (Friday 18 and Monday 21 April are public holidays) Half-Term: Tuesday 27 to Friday 30 May (Monday 26 May is a public holiday) Summer: Starts 23 July All dates are inclusive and are taken from the Norfolk County Council website.

details.outwardRules: Valid on any train timed to arrive at London Liverpool Street (or London Kings Cross or London St Pancras International from East Anglia via Cambridge) before 04:30 and after 09:59, and at Stratford (London) after 09:44 and Tottenham Hale after 09:45.
details.returnRules: Valid on any train departing London Liverpool Street (or London Kings Cross or passing through London St Pancras International to East Anglia via Cambridge) after 09:29, except to Ipswich and beyond (or to Brandon and beyond via Cambridge) between 16:31 and 18:35. Valid on north- or eastbound trains departing Stratford (London) after 09:29 except to Ipswich and beyond (or to Brandon and beyond via Cambridge) between 16:36 and 18:43. Valid on northbound trains departing Tottenham Hale after 09:29 except, to Brandon and beyond via Cambridge, between 16:41 and 18:46.

name: 70min Flex
notes: If intending to make use of the flexibility offered by your ticket, it is recommended that you digitally amend your booking at www.lner.co.uk. If unable to amend your booking, you may still travel via alternative services as defined by the validity of your ticket. You may only travel via alternative services beyond this validity where there is ticket acceptance in place or where you have missed a connection due to earlier delays to your journey. On-board validation equipment is programmed to assume you boarded the train at the origin of your ticket if the train serves that location. If you boarded the train later, you will need to inform onboard staff so that ticket validation returns the correct response.

details.outwardRules: Customers can either travel as booked, or draw on the freedom to start the journey up to 70 minutes earlier or later than originally scheduled when they first purchased the ticket. 'Break of journey' is not permitted under the terms and conditions of your ticket. Customers must travel such that their scheduled arrival time is within 180 minutes (3 hours) of the original booked arrival time. For full terms and conditions, see here . Tickets are not valid with Lumo, Grand Central, Hull Trains or Avanti West Coast.
details.returnRules: Customers should purchase a separate single ticket for return travel.

Plymouth to Liskeard Closure Monday 2 to Friday 6 December 2024 The 08:03 service from London Paddington to Plymouth temporarily becomes Off-Peak for customers travelling to Menheniot, Liskeard, Bodmin and stations on the Looe Branch, only. The 09:04 service from London Paddington to Plymouth temporarily becomes Off-Peak for customers travelling to Menheniot and beyond. The 17:04 service from London Paddington to Plymouth also temporarily becomes Off-Peak for customers travelling to Menheniot and beyond. Travel Towards London and the Southeast Easements allow earlier travel on trains and buses from Cornwall, to maintain connections with the usual Off-Peak services at Plymouth.

details.outwardRules: Not valid on trains timed to depart London Euston before 09:15 or London St Pancras International before 09:00. The n-Network Super Off-Peak (evening single/return fare) is a flat fare generally available after 18:30 but on this journey an easement applies allowing travel from 18:22 within the n-Network (Network West Midlands PTE) area. It is available: Between any two stations in the Network West Midlands area but only after 18:30 Mondays to Sundays. Return journeys must be made the same evening as the outward journey. Available to adults, children and groups of 4 (children will be allowed as a part of the group as long as at least one adult is in the group). The group must travel together as only one ticket will be issued. This fare is not available to unaccompanied children. Period of validity as per the date of travel shown on the ticket. n-Network Super Off-Peak fares are available from staffed Ticket Offices and on-train when the customer has boarded at an unstaffed station. n-Network Super Off-Peak fares cannot be purchased for travel on the last running rail service of the day. No Railcard discounts allowed.

name: FK
notes: If intending to make use of the flexibility offered by your ticket, it is recommended that you digitally amend your booking at www.lner.co.uk. If unable to amend your booking, you may still travel via alternative services as defined by the validity of your ticket. You may only travel via alternative services beyond this validity where there is ticket acceptance in place or where you have missed a connection due to earlier delays to your journey. On-board validation equipment is programmed to assume you boarded the train at the origin of your ticket if the train serves that location. If you boarded the train later, you will need to inform onboard staff so that ticket validation returns the correct response.

details.outwardRules: Customers can either travel as booked, or draw on the freedom to start the journey up to 70 minutes earlier or later than originally scheduled when they first purchased the ticket. 'Break of journey' is not permitted under the terms and conditions of your ticket. Customers must travel such that their scheduled arrival time is within 180 minutes (3 hours) of the original booked arrival time. For full terms and conditions, see here . Tickets are not valid with Lumo, Grand Central, Hull Trains or Avanti West Coast.
details.returnRules: Customers should purchase a separate single ticket for return travel.

details.outwardRules: Valid on London North Eastern Railway only
details.returnRules: Valid on London North Eastern Railway only

details.outwardRules: The n-Network add-on season ticket can only be used for travel when an accompanying valid n-Network ticket is held. The n-Network add-on provides rail travel only from selected stations to the nearest point in the West Midlands county by any permitted route. The ticket restrictions applicable to the n-Network ticket also apply to the n-Network add-on. n-Network Add-on tickets are not valid in conjunction with n-Train tickets. It is valid for travel: On all train companies' services on the permitted routes. Using the same restrictions applicable to the corresponding n-Network ticket. On rail services only. All day during the period of validity. Available from selected stations in the n-Network.
details.returnRules: The n-Network add-on season ticket can only be used for travel when an accompanying valid n-Network ticket is held. details.outwardRules: Not valid on trains timed to depart after 04:29 and before 09:30 or after 16:00 and before 18:30. The restriction after 16:00 and before 18:30 includes any connecting train departing during this period (or re-commencing your journey following a break of journey) within the following areas: Transport for Greater Manchester area plus Alderley Edge, Birchwood, Burscough Bridge, Disley, Handforth, Hoscar, New Mills Central, New Mills Newtown, Padgate, Parbold, Styal, Warrington Bank Quay, Warrington Central, and Wilmslow; Travel South Yorkshire area plus, Darton, Denby Dale, Moorthorpe and South Elmsall; West Yorkshire Metro area plus Cononley, Harrogate, Hornbeam Park, Knaresborough, Pannal, Skipton, Starbeck and Weeton. details.outwardRules: Not valid on trains timed to arrive at other London Terminals or Kensington Olympia after 04:29 and before 10:00, except 07:41 from Heyford 07:45 from Tackley Not valid for change of trains or break of journey between Heyford / Tackley and London Paddington. Boarding anywhere other than Heyford or Tackley with tickets from Heyford or Tackley is not permitted on this service. If Off-peak tickets from Heyford or Tackley include a travelcard, the travelcard element will not be valid for onward travel from London Paddington on the underground, buses or Elizabeth Line, until 09:30. All times are inclusive
details.returnRules: Not valid on trains timed to arrive at other London Terminals or Kensington Olympia after 04:29 and before 10:00, except 07:41 from Heyford 07:45 from Tackley Not valid for change of trains or break of journey between Heyford / Tackley and London Paddington.
